2603.23234.title: MemCollab: Cross-Agent Memory Collaboration via Contrastive Trajectory Distillation
2603.23234.paper_summary: Large language model (LLM)-based agents rely on memory mechanisms to reuse knowledge from past problem-solving experiences. Existing approaches typically construct memory in a per-agent manner, tightly coupling stored knowledge to a single model's reasoning style. In modern deployments with heterogeneous agents, a natural question arises: can a single memory system be shared across different models? We found that naively transferring memory between agents often degrades performance, as such memory entangles task-relevant knowledge with agent-specific biases. To address this challenge, we propose MemCollab, a collaborative memory framework that constructs agent-agnostic memory by contrasting reasoning trajectories generated by different agents on the same task. This contrastive process distills abstract reasoning constraints that capture shared task-level invariants while suppressing agent-specific artifacts. We further introduce a task-aware retrieval mechanism that conditions memory access on task category, ensuring that only relevant constraints are used at inference time. Experiments on mathematical reasoning and code generation benchmarks demonstrate that MemCollab consistently improves both accuracy and inference-time efficiency across diverse agents, including cross-modal-family settings. Our results show that the collaboratively constructed memory can function as a shared reasoning resource for diverse LLM-based agents.
2603.23234.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLM）基礎的代理依賴於記憶機制，以重用過去問題解決經驗中的知識。現有的方法通常以每個代理的方式構建記憶，將存儲的知識與單一模型的推理風格緊密耦合。在具有異質代理的現代部署中，自然會產生一個問題：是否可以在不同模型之間共享單一記憶系統？我們發現，天真地在代理之間轉移記憶往往會降低性能，因為這樣的記憶將與任務相關的知識與代理特定的偏見糾纏在一起。為了解決這一挑戰，我們提出了MemCollab，一個通過對比不同代理在相同任務上生成的推理軌跡來構建與代理無關的記憶的協作記憶框架。這一對比過程提煉出捕捉共享任務級不變性的抽象推理約束，同時抑制代理特定的工件。我們進一步介紹了一種任務感知檢索機制，該機制根據任務類別來調節記憶訪問，確保在推理時僅使用相關的約束。在數學推理和代碼生成基準上的實驗顯示，MemCollab在各種代理中持續提高了準確性和推理時間效率，包括跨模態家族設置。我們的結果表明，協作構建的記憶可以作為多樣化LLM基礎代理的共享推理資源。
2603.23234.author: Yurui Chang et.al.
2603.23234.authors: Yurui Chang, Yiran Wu, Qingyun Wu, Lu Lin
